### TransactionAccessor

any

Subset of DataAccessor methods available inside a transaction callback. Write-only — reads use the outer accessor (snapshot isolation).

Members:

#### upsertSingleTask()

(task: Task) => Promise<void>

#### archiveSingleTask()

(taskId: string, fields: ArchiveFields) => Promise<void>

#### removeSingleTask()

(taskId: string) => Promise<void>

#### setMetaValue()

(key: string, value: unknown) => Promise<void>

#### updateTaskFields()

(taskId: string, fields: TaskFieldUpdates) => Promise<void>

#### appendLog()

(entry: Record<string, unknown>) => Promise<void>

### DataAccessor

any

DataAccessor interface.  Core modules call these methods instead of readJson/saveJson. Each method maps directly to the file-level operations that core modules already perform.

Members:

#### engine

"sqlite"

The storage engine backing this accessor.

#### loadArchive()

() => Promise<ArchiveFile | null>

Load the archive file. Returns null if archive doesn't exist.

#### saveArchive()

(data: ArchiveFile) => Promise<void>

Save the archive file atomically. Creates backup before write.

#### loadSessions()

() => Promise<Session[]>

Load all sessions from the store. Returns empty array if none exist.

#### saveSessions()

(sessions: Session[]) => Promise<void>

Save all sessions to the store atomically.

#### appendLog()

(entry: Record<string, unknown>) => Promise<void>

Append an entry to the audit log.

#### close()

() => Promise<void>

Release any resources (close DB connections, etc.).

#### upsertSingleTask()

(task: Task) => Promise<void>

Upsert a single task (targeted write, no full-file reload).

#### archiveSingleTask()

(taskId: string, fields: ArchiveFields) => Promise<void>

Archive a single task by ID (sets status='archived' + archive metadata).

#### removeSingleTask()

(taskId: string) => Promise<void>

Delete a single task permanently from the tasks table.

#### loadSingleTask()

(taskId: string) => Promise<Task | null>

Load a single task by ID with its dependencies and relations. Returns null if not found.

#### addRelation()

(taskId: string, relatedTo: string, relationType: string, reason?: string | undefined) => Promise<void>

Insert a row into the task_relations table (T5168).

#### getMetaValue()

<T>(key: string) => Promise<T | null>

Read a typed value from the metadata store. Returns null if not found.

#### setMetaValue()

(key: string, value: unknown) => Promise<void>

Write a typed value to the metadata store.

#### getSchemaVersion()

() => Promise<string | null>

Read the schema version from metadata. Convenience for getMetaValue('schema_version').

#### queryTasks()

(filters: TaskQueryFilters) => Promise<QueryTasksResult>

Query tasks with filters, pagination, and ordering. Returns matching tasks + total count.

#### countTasks()

(filters?: { status?: "cancelled" | "pending" | "active" | "blocked" | "done" | "archived" | ("cancelled" | "pending" | "active" | "blocked" | "done" | "archived")[] | undefined; parentId?: string | undefined; } | undefined) => Promise<...>

Count tasks matching optional filters. Excludes archived by default.

#### getChildren()

(parentId: string) => Promise<Task[]>

Get direct children of a parent task.

#### countChildren()

(parentId: string) => Promise<number>

Count direct children of a parent task (all statuses except archived).

#### countActiveChildren()

(parentId: string) => Promise<number>

Count active (non-terminal) children of a parent task.

#### getAncestorChain()

(taskId: string) => Promise<Task[]>

Get ancestor chain from task to root via WITH RECURSIVE CTE. Ordered root-first.

#### getSubtree()

(rootId: string) => Promise<Task[]>

Get full subtree rooted at taskId via WITH RECURSIVE CTE. Includes root.

#### getDependents()

(taskId: string) => Promise<Task[]>

Get tasks that depend on (are blocked by) the given task. Reverse dep lookup.

#### getDependencyChain()

(taskId: string) => Promise<string[]>

Get transitive dependency chain via WITH RECURSIVE CTE. Returns task IDs.

#### taskExists()

(taskId: string) => Promise<boolean>

Check if a task exists (any status including archived).

#### loadTasks()

(taskIds: string[]) => Promise<Task[]>

Load multiple tasks by ID in a single batch query.

#### updateTaskFields()

(taskId: string, fields: TaskFieldUpdates) => Promise<void>

Update specific fields on a task without full load/save cycle.

#### getNextPosition()

(parentId: string | null) => Promise<number>

Get next available position for a task within a parent scope (SQL-level, race-safe).

#### shiftPositions()

(parentId: string | null, fromPosition: number, delta: number) => Promise<void>

Shift positions of siblings = fromPosition by delta (bulk SQL update).

#### transaction()

<T>(fn: (tx: TransactionAccessor) => Promise<T>) => Promise<T>

Execute a function inside a SQLite transaction (BEGIN IMMEDIATE / COMMIT / ROLLBACK).

#### getActiveSession()

() => Promise<Session | null>

Get the currently active session (status='active', most recent).

#### upsertSingleSession()

(session: Session) => Promise<void>

Upsert a single session (targeted write).

#### removeSingleSession()

(sessionId: string) => Promise<void>

Remove a single session by ID.

### AdapterManifest

any

Members:

#### id

string

#### name

string

#### version

string

#### description

string

#### provider

string

Provider identifier, e.g. "claude-code", "opencode", "cursor"

string

### CLEOSpawnContext

any

#### taskId

string

string

CAAMP-compatible spawn options

string | undefined

### CLEOSpawnResult

any

CLEO spawn result Extends CAAMP SpawnResult with CLEO-specific timing and metadata

Members:

#### taskId

string

Task ID that was spawned

#### providerId

string

Provider ID used for the spawn

#### timing

{ startTime: string; endTime?: string | undefined; durationMs?: number | undefined; }

Timing information for the spawn operation

#### manifestEntryId

string | undefined

Reference to manifest entry if output was captured

### CLEOSpawnAdapter

any

Spawn adapter interface Wraps CAAMP SpawnAdapter with CLEO-specific context and result types

Members:

#### id

string

Unique identifier for this adapter instance

#### providerId

string

Provider ID this adapter uses

#### canSpawn()

() => Promise<boolean>

Check if this adapter can spawn in the current environment

Returns: Promise resolving to true if spawning is possible

#### spawn()

(context: CLEOSpawnContext) => Promise<CLEOSpawnResult>

Execute a spawn using the provider's native mechanism

Parameters:
- context: Fully-resolved spawn context

Returns: Promise resolving to spawn result

#### listRunning()

() => Promise<CLEOSpawnResult[]>

List currently running spawns

Returns: Promise resolving to array of running spawn results

#### terminate()

(instanceId: string) => Promise<void>

Terminate a running spawn

Parameters:
- instanceId: ID of the spawn instance to terminate

Returns: Promise that resolves when termination is complete

Human-readable list of found legacy paths.

number

string[]

string

string | undefined

boolean | undefined

boolean | undefined

### TasksAPI

any

Members:

#### add()

(params: { title: string; description: string; parent?: string | undefined; priority?: TaskPriority | undefined; type?: TaskType | undefined; size?: TaskSize | undefined; phase?: string | undefined; labels?: string[] | undefined; depends?: string[] | undefined; notes?: string | undefined; }) => Promise<...>

#### find()

(params: { query?: string | undefined; id?: string | undefined; status?: "cancelled" | "pending" | "active" | "blocked" | "done" | "archived" | undefined; limit?: number | undefined; }) => Promise<...>

#### list()

(params: { taskId: string; title?: string | undefined; status?: "cancelled" | "pending" | "active" | "blocked" | "done" | "archived" | undefined; priority?: TaskPriority | undefined; description?: string | undefined; notes?: string | undefined; }) => Promise<...>

(params: { taskId: string; notes?: string | undefined; }) => Promise<unknown>

#### delete()

(params: { taskId: string; force?: boolean | undefined; }) => Promise<unknown>

#### archive()

(params?: { before?: string | undefined; taskIds?: string[] | undefined; dryRun?: boolean | undefined; } | undefined) => Promise<unknown>

### PinoLogEntry

any

A parsed pino log entry from a CLEO log file. Core fields are always present; additional fields are captured in `extra`.

Members:

#### level

PinoLevel

Uppercase log level

#### time

string

ISO 8601 UTC timestamp

#### pid

number

Process ID

#### hostname

string

Machine hostname

#### msg

string

Human-readable log message

#### subsystem

string | undefined

Logical subsystem name (from child logger)

#### code

string | undefined

CLEO error code (on warn/error entries)

#### exitCode

number | undefined

Numeric exit code (on warn/error entries)

#### extra

Record<string, unknown>

Any additional fields not in the core schema

### LogFileInfo

any

Metadata about a discovered log file.

Members:

#### path

string

Absolute path to the log file

#### name

string

File name (e.g., 'cleo.2026-02-28.1.log')

#### size

number

File size in bytes

#### mtime

string

Last modification time (ISO string)

#### date

string | null

Parsed date from filename, or null if unparseable

#### isActive

boolean

Whether this is the currently active (latest) log file

### LogFilter

any

Filter criteria for log queries. All fields are optional; when multiple are provided, they are ANDed.

Members:

#### minLevel

PinoLevel | undefined

Minimum log level (inclusive). E.g., 'WARN' returns WARN + ERROR + FATAL

#### level

PinoLevel | undefined

Exact log level match

#### since

string | undefined

Start time (inclusive, ISO 8601)

#### until

string | undefined

End time (inclusive, ISO 8601)

#### subsystem

string | undefined

Filter by subsystem name (exact match)

#### code

string | undefined

Filter by CLEO error code (exact match)

#### exitCode

number | undefined

Filter by exit code (exact match)

#### msgContains

string | undefined

Text search in msg field (case-insensitive substring)

#### pid

number | undefined

Filter by PID

#### limit

number | undefined

Maximum entries to return

#### offset

number | undefined

Number of entries to skip (for pagination)

### LogQueryResult

any

Result of a log query operation.

Members:

#### entries

PinoLogEntry[]

Matched entries

#### totalScanned

number

Total entries scanned (before limit/offset)

#### totalMatched

number

Total entries matching filters (before limit/offset)

#### files

string[]

Files that were read

### LogDiscoveryOptions

any

Options for discovering log files.

Members:

#### scope

"both" | "global" | "project" | undefined

Which log directory to scan: 'project', 'global', or 'both' (default: 'project')

#### since

string | undefined

Only include files modified after this date (ISO 8601)

#### includeMigration

boolean | undefined

Include migration log files (default: false)

### LogSummary

any

Summary of log activity across files.

Members:

#### totalEntries

number

#### byLevel

Record<string, number>

#### bySubsystem

Record<string, number>

#### dateRange

{ earliest: string; latest: string; } | null

#### files

LogFileInfo[]

#### name

string

#### url

string

### AgentRegistration

any

string

string

### MessageResult

Result of sending a message.

#### messageId

string

#### conversationId

string

#### status

"pending" | "delivered"

### AgentTransport

any

Provider-neutral interface for inter-agent communication.  Implementations: - SignalDockTransport: HTTP client for SignalDock REST API (provider-neutral) - ClaudeCodeTransport: Wrapper around Claude Code SDK SendMessage (provider-specific)

Members:

#### name

string

Transport name for logging and diagnostics.

#### register()

(name: string, agentClass: AgentClass, privacyTier: PrivacyTier) => Promise<AgentRegistration>

Register an agent with the transport layer.

#### deregister()

(agentId: string) => Promise<void>

Deregister an agent from the transport layer.

#### send()

(fromAgentId: string, toAgentId: string, content: string, conversationId?: string | undefined) => Promise<MessageResult>

Send a message to another agent.

#### poll()

(agentId: string, since?: string | undefined) => Promise<Message[]>

Poll for new messages addressed to this agent.

#### heartbeat()

(agentId: string) => Promise<void>

Send a heartbeat to keep the agent connection alive.

#### createConversation()

(participants: string[], visibility?: ConversationVisibility | undefined) => Promise<Conversation>

Create a conversation between agents.

#### getAgent()

(agentId: string) => Promise<Agent | null>

Get agent info by ID.

### SignalDockTransportConfig

any

Configuration for SignalDockTransport.

Members:

#### endpoint

string

Base URL of the SignalDock API server.

#### agentPrefix

string

Prefix for agent names (e.g., "cleo-" - "cleo-orchestrator").

#### privacyTier

PrivacyTier

Default privacy tier for registered agents.

#### enabled

"http" | "native"

string

string

#### privacyTier

"public" | "discoverable" | "private"

Members:

#### engine

#### createTask()

(task: Task) => Promise<Task>

#### getTask()

(taskId: string) => Promise<Task | null>

#### updateTask()

(taskId: string, updates: Partial<Task>) => Promise<Task | null>

#### deleteTask()

(taskId: string) => Promise<boolean>

#### listTasks()

(filters?: TaskFilters | undefined) => Promise<Task[]>

#### findTasks()

(query: string, limit?: number | undefined) => Promise<Task[]>

#### archiveTask()

(taskId: string, reason?: string | undefined) => Promise<boolean>

#### createSession()

(session: Session) => Promise<Session>

#### getSession()

(sessionId: string) => Promise<Session | null>

#### updateSession()

(sessionId: string, updates: Partial<Session>) => Promise<Session | null>

#### listSessions()

(filters?: SessionFilters | undefined) => Promise<Session[]>

#### endSession()

(sessionId: string, note?: string | undefined) => Promise<Session | null>

#### startTaskOnSession()

(sessionId: string, taskId: string) => Promise<void>

#### getCurrentTaskForSession()

(sessionId: string) => Promise<{ taskId: string | null; since: string | null; }>

#### stopTaskOnSession()

(sessionId: string) => Promise<void>

#### close()

() => Promise<void>

#### addTask()

(options: AddTaskOptions) => Promise<AddTaskResult>

Add a task with full validation, ID generation, and logging.

#### completeTask()

(options: CompleteTaskOptions) => Promise<CompleteTaskResult>

Complete a task with dependency checks and optional auto-completion.

#### richUpdateTask()

(options: UpdateTaskOptions) => Promise<UpdateTaskResult>

Update a task with rich options (addLabels, removeDepends, etc.).

#### showTask()

(taskId: string) => Promise<Task>

Show a task by ID (throws CleoError if not found).

#### richDeleteTask()

(options: DeleteTaskOptions) => Promise<DeleteTaskResult>

Delete a task with force/cascade options.

#### richFindTasks()

(options: FindTasksOptions) => Promise<FindTasksResult>

Find tasks with fuzzy/ID/exact search and filtering.

#### richListTasks()

(options: ListTasksOptions) => Promise<ListTasksResult>

List tasks with full filtering and pagination.

#### richArchiveTasks()

(options: ArchiveTasksOptions) => Promise<ArchiveTasksResult>

Archive tasks in batch with filtering options.

#### startSession()

(options: { name: string; scope: string; autoStart?: boolean | undefined; startTask?: string | undefined; agent?: string | undefined; }) => Promise<Session>

Start a new session with scope, auto-start, etc.

#### richEndSession()

(options?: { sessionId?: string | undefined; note?: string | undefined; } | undefined) => Promise<Session>

End a session, optionally by ID with a note.

#### sessionStatus()

() => Promise<Session | null>

Get the current active session status.

#### resumeSession()

(sessionId: string) => Promise<Session>

Resume a previously ended session.

#### richListSessions()

(options?: { status?: string | undefined; limit?: number | undefined; } | undefined) => Promise<Session[]>

List sessions with status/limit filters.

#### gcSessions()

(maxAgeHours?: number | undefined) => Promise<{ orphaned: string[]; removed: string[]; }>

Garbage collect old sessions.

#### currentTask()

() => Promise<TaskCurrentResult>

Show current task work state.

#### startTask()

(taskId: string) => Promise<TaskStartResult>

Start working on a task by ID.

#### stopTask()

() => Promise<{ previousTask: string | null; }>

Stop working on the current task.

#### getWorkHistory()

() => Promise<TaskWorkHistoryEntry[]>

Get task work history.

#### listLabels()

() => Promise<{ label: string; count: number; statuses: Record<string, number>; }[]>

List all labels with task counts.

#### showLabelTasks()

(label: string) => Promise<Record<string, unknown>>

Show tasks with a specific label.

#### getLabelStats()

() => Promise<Record<string, unknown>>

Get detailed label statistics.

#### suggestRelated()

(taskId: string, opts?: { threshold?: number | undefined; } | undefined) => Promise<Record<string, unknown>>

Suggest related tasks based on shared attributes.

#### addRelation()

(from: string, to: string, type: string, reason: string) => Promise<Record<string, unknown>>

Add a relationship between two tasks.

#### discoverRelated()

(taskId: string) => Promise<Record<string, unknown>>

Discover related tasks using various methods.

#### listRelations()

(taskId: string) => Promise<Record<string, unknown>>

List existing relations for a task.

#### analyzeTaskPriority()

(opts?: { autoStart?: boolean | undefined; } | undefined) => Promise<AnalysisResult>

Analyze task priority with leverage scoring.

"research" | "consensus" | "architecture_decision" | "specification" | "decomposition" | "implementation" | "validation" | "testing" | "release"

Date

Date

boolean

string | undefined

number

{ id: string; version: number; updatedAt: string | null; status: "completed" | "failed" | "cancelled" | "active" | "blocked" | "aborted"; completedAt: string | null; taskId: string; startedAt: string; currentStageId: string | null; }

#### stageRecord

{ id: string; status: "completed" | "failed" | "blocked" | "not_started" | "in_progress" | "skipped"; notesJson: string | null; completedAt: string | null; createdBy: string | null; ... 13 more ...; provenanceChainJson: string | null; }

### AtomicityResult

any

Members:

#### score

number

#### passed

boolean

#### violations

string[]

### RelatesEntry

any

A single relates entry.

Members:

#### taskId

string

#### type

RelatesType

#### reason

string | undefined

### DeleteWarning

any

An impact warning.

Members:

#### severity

Severity

#### code

string

#### message

string

### AffectedTasks

any

Members:

#### primary

Pick<Task, "id" | "status" | "title" | "type" | "parentId" | "labels"> | null

#### children

Pick<Task, "id" | "status" | "title" | "type" | "parentId" | "labels">[]

#### totalCount

number

#### error

string | undefined

any

#### pendingLost

number

#### activeLost

number

#### blockedLost

number

#### doneLost

number

#### dependentsAffected

string[]

#### success

boolean

#### dryRun

true

#### wouldDelete

AffectedTasks | undefined

#### impact

#### warnings

#### warningCount

number | undefined

#### strategy

string | undefined

#### reason

string | null | undefined

#### timestamp

string | undefined

#### error

{ code: string; message: string; suggestion?: string | undefined; childCount?: number | undefined; } | undefined

### ReparentOptions

any

Options for reparenting a task.

Members:

#### taskId

string

#### newParentId

string | null

New parent ID, or null to promote to root.

#### policy

HierarchyPolicy | undefined

Optional resolved hierarchy policy. If not provided, uses llm-agent-first defaults.

### ReparentResult

any

Members:

#### oldParent

string | null

#### newParent

string | null

#### newType

string

### StalenessThresholds

any

Staleness thresholds in days.

Members:

#### stale

number

Days before a task is considered stale.

#### critical

number

Days before a task is critically stale.

#### abandoned

number

Days before a task is considered abandoned.

## Types

### Gateway

any

CQRS gateway: read-only queries vs state-modifying mutations.

### Source

any

Where the request originated.

### Tier

any

Progressive disclosure tier. 0 = tasks + session (80% of agents) 1 = + memory + check (15% of agents) 2 = + pipeline + orchestrate + tools + admin + nexus (5%)

### ParamType

any

The concrete value types a parameter can carry at runtime. Drives JSON Schema `type` and Commander argument/option parsing.

### CanonicalDomain

any

### DispatchNext

any

Async function that produces a DispatchResponse.

### Middleware

any

Middleware function signature.  Receives the request and a `next` continuation. Can short-circuit by returning early (e.g., rate-limit exceeded) or modify the request/response.

### RuntimeData

any

### JobStatus

any

Background job status

### MviTier

any

MVI (Minimum Viable Information) projection configurations. Maps disclosure tiers to operation access and field filtering rules.   T4820  T5096

### ReleaseFn

### ProviderHookEvent

any

CAAMP-defined hook events supported by provider capability discovery.

### InternalHookEvent

any

### HookEvent

any

Full CLEO hook event union.  CAAMP defines provider-facing events; CLEO extends the registry with local coordination events for autonomous execution.

### HookHandler

any

Handler function type for hook events Handlers receive project root and typed payload

### CLEOLifecycleEvent

any

Type for CLEO lifecycle event names These are the internal events CLEO fires that get mapped to CAAMP events

### CLEOAutonomousLifecycleEvent

any

Type for autonomous CLEO lifecycle events.

### EvidenceType

any

### Stage

any

Stage type derived from canonical stage list.   T4800

### StageCategory

any

Stage category for grouping related stages.   T4800

### EnforcementMode

any

Lifecycle enforcement modes.

### AlertLevel

any

Alert level names.

### GatewayMetaRecord

any

GatewayMeta with an index signature for DomainResponse._meta compatibility.  All domain handlers receive this from createGatewayMeta().   T4655

### ActionHandler

any

Type for the action handler function - flexible to support various signatures

### MutateResponse

any

Mutate response interface (aliases DomainResponse)

### QueryResponse

any

Query response interface (aliases DomainResponse)

### ExternalTaskStatus

any

Normalized status for tasks coming from an external provider.

### ConflictPolicy

any

Policy for resolving conflicts between CLEO and provider state.  - `cleo-wins`: CLEO state takes precedence (default). - `provider-wins`: Provider state takes precedence. - `latest-wins`: Most recently modified value wins. - `report-only`: Report conflicts without applying changes.

### ReconcileActionType

any

The type of action the reconciliation engine will take.

### TaskStatus

any

### SessionStatus

any

### PipelineStatus

any

### StageStatus

any

### AdrStatus

any

### GateStatus

any

### ManifestStatus

any

### EntityType

any

### TaskPriority

any

Task priority levels.

### TaskType

any

Task type in hierarchy.

### TaskSize

any

Task size (scope, NOT time).

### EpicLifecycle

any

Epic lifecycle states.

### TaskOrigin

any

Task origin (provenance).

### VerificationAgent

any

Verification agent types.

### VerificationGate

any

Verification gate names.

### CompletedTask

any

A task with `status = 'done'`. Narrows `Task` to require `completedAt`.  Use this type when you need to guarantee a completed task has its completion timestamp — for example, in cycle-time calculations or archive operations.

### CancelledTask

any

A task with `status = 'cancelled'`. Narrows `Task` to require `cancelledAt` and `cancellationReason`.  Use this type when processing cancelled tasks where the cancellation metadata is guaranteed to be present.

### PhaseStatus

any

Phase status.

### ReleaseStatus

any

Release status.

### ArchiveReportType

any

Report type for archive statistics.

### OutputFormat

any

Output format options.

### DateFormat

any

Date format options.

any

Hierarchy enforcement profile preset.

### LogLevel

any

Pino log levels.

any

Lifecycle enforcement mode.

any

Sharing mode: whether .cleo/ files are committed to the project git repo.

any

Unified CLEO response envelope.  Every CLEO response (CLI or MCP) is a CleoResponse. MCP responses include the _meta field; CLI responses do not.

## Enums

### ExitCode

typeof ExitCode

CLEO exit codes — canonical definitions shared across all layers.  Ranges: 0 = success, 1-99 = errors, 100+ = special (non-error) states.   T4454  T4456  T5710

Members:

#### SUCCESS

ExitCode.SUCCESS

#### GENERAL_ERROR

ExitCode.GENERAL_ERROR

#### INVALID_INPUT

### ProtocolExitCode

typeof ProtocolExitCode

Protocol-specific exit codes used by the RCASD-IVTR+C enforcement system.  These map to the protocol violation range (60-70) and lifecycle enforcement range (80-84) defined in the MCP server specification. The values here align with the protocol enforcement layer's own exit code semantics, which differ from the canonical CLI exit codes in src/types/exit-codes.ts.  The canonical CLI ExitCode enum at src/types/exit-codes.ts maps range 60-67 to orchestrator errors, while this enum maps them to protocol violations. Both are valid in their respective contexts — CLI vs protocol enforcement.

Members:

#### SUCCESS

ProtocolExitCode.SUCCESS

#### E_GENERAL_ERROR

ProtocolExitCode.E_GENERAL_ERROR

#### E_INVALID_INPUT

ProtocolExitCode.E_INVALID_INPUT

#### E_FILE_ERROR

ProtocolExitCode.E_FILE_ERROR

#### E_NOT_FOUND

ProtocolExitCode.E_NOT_FOUND

#### E_DEPENDENCY_ERROR

ProtocolExitCode.E_DEPENDENCY_ERROR

#### E_VALIDATION_ERROR

ProtocolExitCode.E_VALIDATION_ERROR

#### E_PARENT_NOT_FOUND

ProtocolExitCode.E_PARENT_NOT_FOUND

#### E_DEPTH_EXCEEDED

ProtocolExitCode.E_DEPTH_EXCEEDED

#### E_SIBLING_LIMIT

ProtocolExitCode.E_SIBLING_LIMIT

#### E_CIRCULAR_REFERENCE

ProtocolExitCode.E_CIRCULAR_REFERENCE

#### E_SESSION_REQUIRED

ProtocolExitCode.E_SESSION_REQUIRED

#### E_PROTOCOL_RESEARCH

ProtocolExitCode.E_PROTOCOL_RESEARCH

#### E_PROTOCOL_CONSENSUS

ProtocolExitCode.E_PROTOCOL_CONSENSUS

#### E_PROTOCOL_SPECIFICATION

ProtocolExitCode.E_PROTOCOL_SPECIFICATION

#### E_PROTOCOL_DECOMPOSITION

ProtocolExitCode.E_PROTOCOL_DECOMPOSITION

#### E_PROTOCOL_IMPLEMENTATION

ProtocolExitCode.E_PROTOCOL_IMPLEMENTATION

#### E_PROTOCOL_CONTRIBUTION

ProtocolExitCode.E_PROTOCOL_CONTRIBUTION

#### E_PROTOCOL_RELEASE

ProtocolExitCode.E_PROTOCOL_RELEASE

#### E_PROTOCOL_GENERIC

ProtocolExitCode.E_PROTOCOL_GENERIC

#### E_PROTOCOL_VALIDATION

ProtocolExitCode.E_PROTOCOL_VALIDATION

#### E_TESTS_SKIPPED

ProtocolExitCode.E_TESTS_SKIPPED

#### E_LIFECYCLE_GATE_FAILED

ProtocolExitCode.E_LIFECYCLE_GATE_FAILED

### CANONICAL_DOMAINS

readonly ["tasks", "session", "memory", "check", "pipeline", "orchestrate", "tools", "admin", "nexus", "sticky"]

The 10 canonical domain names.

### OPERATIONS

OperationDef[]

The single source of truth for all operations in CLEO.

### STRING_TO_EXIT

Record<string, number>

Canonical mapping from string error codes to numeric exit codes.  Source of truth: src/types/exit-codes.ts (ExitCode enum). Must stay in sync with ERROR_CODE_TO_EXIT in src/dispatch/adapters/cli.ts.

### INTERNAL_HOOK_EVENTS

readonly ["onWorkAvailable", "onAgentSpawn", "onAgentComplete", "onCascadeStart", "onPatrol"]

CLEO-local coordination events used by the autonomous runtime.  These are internal lifecycle signals for worker orchestration and are not surfaced through CAAMP's provider capability registry.

### CLEO_TO_CAAMP_HOOK_MAP

{ readonly 'session.start': "onSessionStart"; readonly 'session.end': "onSessionEnd"; readonly 'task.start': "onToolStart"; readonly 'task.complete': "onToolComplete"; readonly 'file.change': "onFileChange"; readonly 'system.error': "onError"; readonly 'prompt.submit': "onPromptSubmit"; readonly 'response.complete':...

Mapping from CLEO internal lifecycle events to CAAMP hook events This is where CLEO connects its lifecycle to CAAMP's event definitions

### CLEO_INTERNAL_HOOK_MAP

{ readonly 'agent.work.available': "onWorkAvailable"; readonly 'agent.spawn': "onAgentSpawn"; readonly 'agent.complete': "onAgentComplete"; readonly 'cascade.start': "onCascadeStart"; readonly 'watcher.patrol': "onPatrol"; }

Internal CLEO lifecycle events that drive autonomous coordination.

### hooks

HookRegistry

Singleton instance of the HookRegistry.  Use this instance for all hook operations throughout the application.

### PIPELINE_STAGES

readonly ["research", "consensus", "architecture_decision", "specification", "decomposition", "implementation", "validation", "testing", "release"]

### CONTRIBUTION_STAGE

"contribution"

Cross-cutting contribution stage. Not part of the pipeline execution order, but tracked in the schema for attribution and provenance recording.   T4800

### STAGE_DEFINITIONS

Record<"research" | "consensus" | "architecture_decision" | "specification" | "decomposition" | "implementation" | "validation" | "testing" | "release", StageDefinition>

Canonical stage definitions with complete metadata.   T4800  T4799 - Replaces legacy STAGE_DEFINITIONS from index.ts

### STAGE_ORDER

Record<"research" | "consensus" | "architecture_decision" | "specification" | "decomposition" | "implementation" | "validation" | "testing" | "release", number>

Stage order mapping for quick lookups.   T4800

### STAGE_PREREQUISITES

Record<"research" | "consensus" | "architecture_decision" | "specification" | "decomposition" | "implementation" | "validation" | "testing" | "release", ("research" | "consensus" | ... 6 more ... | "release")[]>

Prerequisites for each stage - which stages must be completed before entering.   T4800  T4799 - Canonical prerequisite map

### TRANSITION_RULES

TransitionRule[]

Allowed transitions between stages.  By default, stages progress linearly. These rules define exceptions.   T4800

### STAGE_COUNT

9

Total number of stages in the pipeline.   T4800

### FIRST_STAGE

"research" | "consensus" | "architecture_decision" | "specification" | "decomposition" | "implementation" | "validation" | "testing" | "release"

First stage in the pipeline.   T4800

### LAST_STAGE

"research" | "consensus" | "architecture_decision" | "specification" | "decomposition" | "implementation" | "validation" | "testing" | "release"

Last stage in the pipeline.   T4800

### PLANNING_STAGES

("research" | "consensus" | "architecture_decision" | "specification" | "decomposition" | "implementation" | "validation" | "testing" | "release")[]

Planning stages.   T4800

### DECISION_STAGES

("research" | "consensus" | "architecture_decision" | "specification" | "decomposition" | "implementation" | "validation" | "testing" | "release")[]

Decision stages.   T4800

### EXECUTION_STAGES

("research" | "consensus" | "architecture_decision" | "specification" | "decomposition" | "implementation" | "validation" | "testing" | "release")[]

Execution stages (canonical).   T4800

### VALIDATION_STAGES

("research" | "consensus" | "architecture_decision" | "specification" | "decomposition" | "implementation" | "validation" | "testing" | "release")[]

### CORE_PROTECTED_FILES

readonly ["config.json", ".gitignore", "project-info.json", "project-context.json"]

Configuration files relative to .cleo/ that MUST remain tracked by project git. These are JSON/text config files only — NOT databases. SQLite databases (tasks.db) are excluded: they must be gitignored to prevent data-loss from merge conflicts (see ADR-013).  If any of these files are gitignored, cleo doctor reports a critical finding.

### VALID_WORKFLOW_GATE_STATUSES

readonly [null, "passed", "failed", "blocked"]

Valid workflow gate status values per Section 7.3

### ROUTING_TABLE

RoutingEntry[]

Static routing table for all canonical operations.  Operations are grouped by domain with channel preferences based on: - MCP: lower overhead (no CLI startup), direct DB access, structured JSON - CLI: human-readable output, shell integration, interactive prompts - Either: no significant difference or context-dependent
